topic.name: Temporary Duty Suspensions
topic.description: The documents discuss temporary suspensions of duties on various materials and products in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on Poly-Pale
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 2: extend temporarily the suspension of duty on nylon woolpacks
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 3: extend the temporary duty suspension on certain rayon staple fibers
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 4: extend the temporary suspension of duty on modacrylic staple fibers
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 5: suspend temporarily the duty on alginic acid
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 6: extend the suspension of duty on staple fibers of rayon
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 7: extend temporarily the suspension of duty on cuprate
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: Small Business Lending Programs
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts and amendments related to enhancing access to capital and loan programs for small businesses, particularly those owned by veterans and other specific groups.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Investment Act of 1958 to authorize the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to make loans directly to small businesses owned and controlled by veterans
Directs the Administrator to reduce any applicable requirement relating to the veteran's credit score.
Document 2: Amends the Small Business Act to increase maximum amounts of loans under the Small Business Administration (SBA) loan programs
Expresses the sense of Congress that the Administrator should establish a website that lists SBA lenders and provides loan rate information.
Document 3: Amends the Small Business Act with respect to the HUBZone program to authorize a contracting opportunity to be awarded under the program on the basis of competition restricted to qualified HUBZone small businesses
Authorizes the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish mentor-protege programs for small businesses.
Document 4: Amends the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 to extend small business loan fee reductions and eliminations
Allows the SBA Administrator to pool SBA-guaranteed loans having various interest rates.
Document 5: Directs the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish and carry out a program to provide support to SBA regional, district, and branch offices
Makes permanent the SBA's Community Express Program.
Document 6: Amends the Small Business Act and the Small Business Investment Act of 1958 to raise the maximum amounts of loans for the benefit of small businesses
Suspends certain SBA loan fees during the period beginning on the date of enactment.
Document 7: Directs the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to collect no fees currently charged by the SBA to borrowers and lenders under the SBA's 7(a) loan program
Increases maximum limits on SBA loans made to small businesses under the Small Business Act.

topic.name: Electronic Communications Privacy
topic.description: Legislation governing the protection and disclosure of electronic communications and geolocation information by service providers to governmental entities.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to authorize a governmental entity to require the disclosure of the contents of any wire or electronic communication only pursuant to a warrant.
Prohibits a governmental entity from intentionally intercepting geolocation information pertaining to an individual.
Document 3: Amends the Electronic Communications Privacy Act of 1986 to prohibit a provider of remote computing service or electronic communication service to the public from knowingly divulging to any governmental entity the contents of any communication.
Requires a law enforcement agency, within 10 days after receiving the contents of a customer's communication, or a governmental entity, within 3 days, to provide the customer a copy of the warrant.

topic.name: Middle Grades Education Improvement
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing educational outcomes for middle school students, particularly those from low-income backgrounds.

implement state middle grades needs analyses and improvement plans
Document 2: directs the Department of Education (ED) to allot grants to states, based on their proportion of poor children aged 5 to 17
implement state middle grades needs analyses and improvement plans
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Education to make grants to states, based on their proportion of poor children aged 5 to 17

Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Education to make grants to states, based on their proportion of poor children aged 5 to 17

Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Education to make grants to states, based on their proportion of poor children aged 5 to 17

topic.name: Tax Incentives for Education and Childcare
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at providing tax benefits and deductions related to education savings, lifelong learning, and childcare expenses.

increase the annual contribution limit for such accounts

establish tax-exempt lifelong learning accounts to pay certain educational expenses

Limits the dollar amount of such deduction in a taxable year to $7,000 for taxpayers with one qualifying child
Document 4: Working Parent Support Act of 2015
deduction from gross income for employment-related expenses incurred in caring for a child

Limits the dollar amount of such deduction in a taxable year to $7,000 for taxpayers with one qualifying child
Document 6: Personal Philanthropy Account Act of 2007
allow a tax deduction for cash contributions to a personal philanthropy account
Document 7: Kids Invest and Develop Savings Act of 2007
increase the allowable amount of the tax credit for retirement savings

topic.name: Great Lakes Environmental Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses federal initiatives and legislation aimed at the conservation, research, and management of marine and freshwater ecosystems, particularly focusing on the Great Lakes.

Document 1: establish a a program to develop a coordinated and comprehensive federal ocean and coastal mapping program for the Great Lakes and coastal state waters
enhances ecosystem approaches in decision-making for conservation and management of marine resources and habitats
Document 3: authorize the U.S. Geological Survey (USGS) to conduct critical monitoring, scientific assessments, and research in support of fisheries within the Great Lakes Basin
execute a comprehensive, multi-lake, freshwater fisheries science program
Document 5: establish an Inter-Agency Task Force on Harmful Algal Blooms and Hypoxia
develop a national strategy to address marine and freshwater harmful algal bloom and hypoxia events
Document 6: provide for the development and coordination of a comprehensive and integrated national program to address harmful algal blooms and hypoxia
facilitate efforts to develop and implement harmful algal bloom and hypoxia response plans, strategies, and tools

topic.name: Tax Credits for Renewable Energy
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code related to tax credits for renewable energy sources and alternative fuel vehicles.

Document 1: increase the rate of such credit from 30% to 50% for hydrogen-related alternative fuel vehicles
extend such credit through 2016 for property related to hydrogen.
Document 2: increase the rate of such credit from 30% to 50% for hydrogen-related alternative fuel vehicles
increase the maximum dollar amount of the energy tax credit for investment in qualified fuel cell property.
Document 3: extend through 2018 the tax credit for producing electricity from certain renewable resources
extends through 2018 the energy tax credit for solar energy, fuel cell, and microturbine property.
Document 4: include a 30% credit for qualified biomass fuel property expenditures
uses the burning of biomass fuel to heat a residence.
Document 5: extends through 2012 the alternative motor vehicles tax credit
increases from 30 to 60% the rate of the tax credit for alternative fuel vehicle refueling property.
Document 6: include a 30% credit for qualified biomass fuel property expenditures
uses the burning of biomass fuel to heat a residence.
Document 7: allow an energy tax credit for investment in equipment that uses natural gas
allow a residential energy efficient property tax credit for 30% of the qualified natural gas heat pump property expenditures.

topic.name: Youth Empowerment and Violence Prevention
topic.description: Legislation aimed at combating gang activity and violence against women, with a focus on empowering youth and providing support services.

Document 1: Authorizes the Attorney General to make grants to public and nonprofit private entities for projects involving innovative approaches to combat gang activity
expand and improve cooperative efforts to address gang activity problems
Document 2: Expands the purposes for which STOP grants may be used to include training of law enforcement personnel and prosecutors
addressing backlogs of sexual assault evidence collection kits
Document 3: Amends the Violence Against Women Act of 1994 to revise definitions under such Act
requires the Director of the Office on Violence Against Women to ensure that entities providing training or technical assistance have demonstrated expertise
Document 6: Allows those grants to be used to address the needs of youth who are victims of, or exposed to, domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, sex trafficking, or stalking
develop and promote best practices for responding to domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, sex trafficking, and stalking

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Eligibility
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the eligibility and benefits of veterans, including medical enrollment and interment in national cemeteries.

Document 1: Deems as active-duty service, for purposes of benefits provided through the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA)
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to automatically enroll in the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) medical system certain veterans who served on active duty in combat operations
Document 3: Redefines the term 'period of war' for purposes of eligibility for veterans' pension benefits
Document 4: Allows members of the Armed Forces not in uniform and veterans to render the military salute during the recitation of the pledge of allegiance
Document 5: Makes eligible for interment in a national cemetery any individual who served in combat support of the Armed Forces in Laos
Document 6: Applies this Act to any qualifying individual who dies on or after January 1, 2011
Document 7: Gives such veterans the option of declining enrollment

topic.name: Abortion Legislation and Parental Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses federal laws regulating abortion practices, particularly concerning minors and parental involvement in abortion decisions.

Document 1: prohibit transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion
imposes a fine and/or prison term of up to one year on a physician who performs or induces an abortion on an out-of-state minor
Document 2: Imposes criminal penalties on anyone who knowingly or knowingly attempts to perform an abortion based on the sex, gender, color or race of the child
Authorizes civil actions for verifiable money damages for injuries and punitive damages
Document 3: prohibit transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion
requires such physician to give 24-hour actual or constructive notice to a parent of the minor seeking an abortion
Document 4: prohibit transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion
makes an exception for an abortion necessary to save the life of the minor
Document 7: defines 'abortion' as the termination of a pregnancy with an intention other than to increase the probability of a live birth
authorizes any parent who suffers harm from a violation of such prohibition to obtain appropriate relief in a civil action

topic.name: Consumer Protection and Aviation Safety
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing consumer protection in broadband services and improving safety standards in aviation.

Document 1: Broadband Service Consumer Protection Act
assist consumers in making realistic assumptions about broadband service speeds
Document 2: Safe Aviation and Flight Emergency Tracking Act of 2015
ensure that a covered aircraft is equipped with technology that provides for the continuous tracking of information regarding the aircraft during flight
Document 3: Safe Aviation and Flight Emergency Tracking Act of 2014
issue regulations to ensure that a covered aircraft is equipped with technology for continuous tracking
Document 4: Safe Aviation and Flight Enhancement Act of 2014
require all commercial passenger aircraft to be equipped with a deployable recorder system
Document 6: Basic Airline Services to Improve Customer Satisfaction Act
require commercial air carriers to allow each air passenger to check one bag and carry on one personal item

topic.name: Wilderness Area Designation
topic.description: The documents discuss the designation of federal lands as wilderness areas and components of the National Wilderness Preservation System in various U.S. regions.

Document 1: Amends the Colorado Wilderness Act of 1993 to designate certain federal lands in the White River National Forest in Colorado as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Designates certain federal lands in such forest, comprising approximately 11,417 acres, as the Tenmile Recreation Management Area.
Document 2: Designates certain federal land in the Olympic National Forest in the state of Washington as wilderness and as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Amends the Wild and Scenic Rivers Act to designate as wild, scenic, or recreational rivers specified segments of the following rivers in the state of Washington.
Document 3: Designates certain federal land in the Olympic National Forest in the state of Washington as wilderness and components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Authorizes the Department of Agriculture to take necessary measures to control fire, insects, and diseases in the wilderness areas designated by this Act.
Document 4: Designates certain federal land in the Olympic National Forest in the state of Washington as wilderness and as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Withdraws the federal land within the boundaries of the designated river segments from: (1) entry, appropriation, or disposal under the public land laws.
Document 5: Designates certain federal land in the Olympic National Forest in the state of Washington as wilderness and as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Withdraws the federal land within the boundaries of the designated river segments from: (1) entry, appropriation, or disposal under the public land laws.
Document 6: Designates specified lands in the Grand Mesa, Uncompahgre, and Gunnison National Forests and the San Juan Resource Area of the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) in Colorado as wilderness areas.
Withdraws lands designated as wilderness or a special management area under this Act from all forms of: (1) entry, appropriation, or disposal under public land laws.
Document 7: Designates specified lands in the Grand Mesa, Uncompahgre, and Gunnison National Forests and specified land of the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) in Colorado as wilderness areas.
Withdraws lands designated as wilderness or a special management area under this Act from: (1) entry, appropriation, and disposal under public land laws.

topic.name: FISA and Intelligence Oversight
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments and reforms related to the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (FISA) and the oversight of intelligence activities in the United States.

Document 1: Authorizes FY2014 appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Requires the DNI to establish guidelines to govern the treatment under such authorized personnel levels.
Document 2: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to add a new title concerning additional procedures for acquiring the communications of certain persons outside the United States.
Requires the targeting to be conducted in a manner consistent with the fourth amendment to the Constitution.
Document 3: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to establish a new process to be followed when the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) submits an application to a FISA court for an order requiring the production of tangible things.
Requires the FBI to include in such tangible thing applications a specific selection term to be used as the basis for production.
Document 4: Requires the head of each federal agency to submit a monthly report to the Administrator of the Office of Information and Regulatory Affairs for each rule such agency expects to propose or finalize during the following year.
Sets forth the required content of such reports, including a summary of the nature of the rule.
Document 5: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to establish a new process to be followed when the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) submits an application to a FISA court for an order requiring the production of business records.
Prohibits the FBI from applying for a tangible thing production order unless a specific selection term is used as the basis for the production.
Document 6: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to establish a new process to be followed when the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) submits an application to a FISA court for an order requiring the production of business records.
Establishes two separate frameworks for the production of tangible things with different standards.
Document 7: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to establish a new process to be followed when the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) submits an application to a FISA court for an order requiring the production of business records.
Prohibits the FBI from applying for a tangible thing production order unless a specific selection term is used as the basis for the production.

topic.name: Water Resources Development and Management
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation and policies related to the development, conservation, and management of water resources in the United States.

facilitate and expedite water transfers in accordance with that Act.
Document 2: authorize projects that are the subject of a completed report of the Chief of Engineers of the Army Corps of Engineers
authorize the Secretary of the Army to review projects that require increased authorizations
Document 3: revises or authorizes U.S. Army Corps of Engineers water resources development projects
establishes a process to deauthorize projects with an aggregate estimated federal cost to complete of at least $10 billion.
Document 4: Reauthorizes the Water Resources Development Act (WRDA)
Authorizes flood control, navigation, and environmental projects and studies by the Army Corps of Engineers.
Document 5: Authorizes Army Corps of Engineers (Corps) water resources development and conservation projects
including flood control, environmental restoration, shoreline protection, and inland navigation projects.
Document 6: amends the Water Resources Development Act of 1986 (WRDA 1986) to allow the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers to provide technical assistance to nonfederal interests
the Corps of Engineers may receive and repay funds from states or political subdivisions for immediate use on authorized water resources development studies or projects.
Document 7: requires the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers to enter cooperative agreements with youth service and conservation corps organizations
the Corps of Engineers must use its existing channel depths and dimensions authority under the Rivers and Harbors Appropriation Act of 1915.

the importance of the deployment of wireless broadband services in rural areas.

authorizes the establishment of a private, nonprofit Public Safety Broadband Corporation to deploy a nationwide, interoperable public safety broadband network.

Dungeness crab fishery for which there is no fishery management plan

provisions concerning commercial aviation accidents also applicable to maritime accidents

clear a blockage of vehicular traffic at a grade crossing
Document 5: extends and modifies the temporary suspension of duty on certain men's footwear
designed to be worn in lieu of other footwear as protection against water, oil, grease, or chemicals

Document 7: amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: Workforce Development and Education

topic.name: SBIR and STTR Programs
topic.description: The documents discuss the reauthorization, funding, and operational aspects of the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs.

Document 1: reauthorize through: (1) FY2022 the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR); and (2) FY2023 the Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs

increases, for both the SBIR and STTR programs, the individual small business award levels from: (1) $100,000 to $150,000, for participation at the Phase I level; and (2) $750,000 to $1 million, for participation at the Phase II level.
Document 3: reauthorize through FY2010 the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs

increases, for both the SBIR and STTR programs, the individual small business award levels from: (1) $100,000 to $300,000, for participation at a Phase One level; and (2) $750,000 to $2.2 million, for participation at a Phase Two level.
Document 5: reauthorize through FY2018 the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs

increases, for both the SBIR and STTR programs, the individual small business award levels from: (1) $100,000 to $150,000, for participation at the Phase I level; and (2) $750,000 to $1 million, for participation at the Phase II level.
Document 6: reauthorize through FY2019 the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs

increases, for both the SBIR and STTR programs, the individual small business award levels from: (1) $100,000 to $150,000, for participation at the Phase I level; and (2) $750,000 to $1 million, for participation at the Phase II level.
Document 7: reauthorize through FY2014 the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs

increases, for both the SBIR and STTR programs, the individual small business award levels from: (1) $100,000 to $150,000, for participation at the Phase I level; and (2) $750,000 to $1 million, for participation at the Phase II level.

topic.name: U.S. Energy Policy and Regulation

streamlining permitting for American energy

determine a domestic strategic production goal
Document 3: repeal the authority of the President to restrict exports of coal, petroleum products, natural gas
grant licenses to export crude oil
Document 4: repeal limitations on oil exports
take into account solely domestic environmental impacts
Document 5: repeal limitations on export of Outer Continental Shelf oil or gas
authorization and approval process for energy infrastructure projects
Document 6: no order of the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission is required to authorize the export of natural gas

FERC to issue a permit within 30 days after receipt of an application

topic.name: Firearm Regulation in D.C.
topic.description: Legislation concerning the ownership, possession, and regulation of firearms in the District of Columbia.

Document 1: Amends specified law prohibiting the killing of wild birds and wild animals in the District of Columbia to declare that nothing in it or any other provision of law shall authorize or be construed to permit the Council, the Mayor, or any governmental or regulatory authority of the District to prohibit, constructively prohibit, or unduly burden the ability of persons otherwise not prohibited from possessing firearms under federal law from acquiring, possessing in their homes or businesses, or using for sporting, self-protection or other lawful purposes, any firearm neither prohibited by federal law nor subject to the National Firearms Act.
Denies the District any authority to enact laws or regulations that discourage or eliminate the private ownership or use of firearms.
Document 3: Amends specified law prohibiting the killing of wild birds and wild animals in the District of Columbia to declare that nothing in it or any other provision of law shall authorize or be construed to permit the Council, the Mayor, or any governmental or regulatory authority of the District to prohibit, constructively prohibit, or unduly burden the ability of persons otherwise not prohibited from possessing firearms under federal law from acquiring, possessing in their homes or businesses, transporting for legitimate purposes, or using for sporting, self-protection or other lawful purposes, any firearm neither prohibited by federal law nor subject to the National Firearms Act.
Declares that nothing in such prohibitions shall be construed to prohibit the District from regulating the carrying of firearms by a person, either concealed or openly.
Document 5: Amends specified law prohibiting the killing of wild birds and wild animals in the District of Columbia to declare that nothing in it or any other provision of law shall authorize or be construed to permit the Council, the Mayor, or any governmental or regulatory authority of the District to prohibit, constructively prohibit, or unduly burden the ability of persons otherwise not prohibited from possessing firearms under federal law from acquiring, possessing in their homes or businesses, transporting for legitimate purposes, or using for sporting, self-protection or other lawful purposes, any firearm neither prohibited by federal law nor subject to the National Firearms Act.
Denies the District any authority to enact laws or regulations that discourage or eliminate the private ownership or use of firearms.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration and National Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses various legislative measures related to U.S. immigration policy, national security, and the protection of human rights.

Document 1: Authorizes the President to deny U.S. entry to an alien who holds a position in the senior leadership of the government of the People's Republic of China.
has participated in the repression or persecution of Tibetans, Uyghurs, Mongolians, or other ethnic minority.
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include among the grounds for loss of U.S. nationality by a native-born or naturalized citizen: taking an oath or making a declaration of allegiance to a foreign terrorist organization.
becoming a member of or providing training or material assistance to any foreign terrorist organization.
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to make inadmissible to the United States an alien who engages in certain sanctionable activities with Iran.
Authorizes presidential waiver of such prohibition under specified conditions.
Document 4: Expresses the sense of the Senate that the United States should encourage extradition and prosecution of the remaining Nazi war criminals.
Directs the Attorney General to consider the extent to which such country is cooperating in extraditing or prosecuting Nazi war criminals.
Document 5: Prohibits the President from releasing or transferring an individual currently detained at Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay, Cuba, to the custody of the individual's country of origin.
the President certifies to Congress that such country is not a designated state sponsor of terrorism.
Document 6: This bill amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to require the President to recommend to Congress the number of refugees who may be admitted into the United States.
the Department of Homeland Security shall consult with the Director of National Intelligence and the Federal Bureau of Investigation.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of State to submit a semiannual report to Congress for five years that identifies U.S. citizens imprisoned in foreign countries whose fundamental due process and human rights are being violated.
Prohibits the Secretary of State from issuing any visa to, and the Secretary of Homeland Security from permitting entry to the United States of, any such identified official.

topic.name: Student Loan Regulations
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving the structure, transparency, and terms of student loans and financial aid in higher education.

Document 1: requires institutions of higher education (IHEs) to notify their Pell Grant recipients
requires exit counseling that IHEs provide to student borrowers to include personalized information

requires the Secretary to forgive students' outstanding principal on such interest-free loans
Document 3: requires the standard form to include certain additional items
requires more detailed information to be included on the form
Document 4: requires the Secretary of Education to discharge a borrower's liability on a loan
provides such liability discharge and eligibility protections to borrowers
Document 5: requires the standard form to include certain additional items
requires more detailed information to be included on the form
Document 6: limits the unpaid amount of Direct Stafford loans that students may amass
caps the annual interest rate for Direct Stafford loans at 6.8%
Document 7: limits the unpaid amount of Direct Stafford loans that students may amass

topic.name: Designation of Federal Properties
topic.description: The documents discuss the designation and conveyance of federal properties and facilities across various locations in the United States.

Document 3: Designates peak 12,240 as Mt. Andrea Lawrence.
Document 5: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 4567 Rockbridge Road as the 'Francis Manuel Ortega Post Office.'
Document 6: Designates the federal building and U.S. courthouse located at 511 East San Antonio Avenue as the 'R. E. Thomason Federal Building and United States Courthouse.'
Document 7: Designates the facility of the Grandfather Ranger District of the U.S. Forest Service as the 'Jason Crisp Forest Service Building.'

topic.name: Clean Air Act Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes and proposals related to the Clean Air Act focusing on air quality standards, emissions regulations, and renewable fuel requirements.

Document 1: Amends the Clean Air Act to require any air quality impact of Outer Continental Shelf (OCS) sources to be measured or modeled

Prohibits the Environmental Appeals Board of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from having any authority to consider any matter regarding the consideration, issuance, or denial of such permit.
Document 2: Delays the implementation of the Environmental Protection Agency's (EPA) 2015 national ambient air quality standards (NAAQS) for ozone
Changes the interval by which the EPA must review its NAAQS for criteria pollutants from a 5-year review cycle to a 10-year review cycle.
Document 3: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel program
Repealing a requirement that the Administrator of EPA promulgate fuel regulations to implement measures to mitigate adverse impacts on air quality.
Document 4: Prohibits the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from taking any action under the Clean Air Act (CAA) with respect to any stationary source permitting requirement
Exempts from such prohibitions any action under the CAA's emission standards for motor vehicles.
Document 5: Extends compliance deadlines for rules under the Clean Air Act that address carbon dioxide emissions from existing fossil fuel-fired power plants
The EPA must treat hydropower as renewable energy when implementing or enforcing the rules.
Document 6: Amends the Clean Air Act to repeal prohibitions against aggregating emissions from any oil or gas exploration or production well
Requires the Administrator to issue a final rule adding hydrogen sulfide to the list of hazardous air pollutants.
Document 7: Repeals prohibitions against aggregating emissions from any oil or gas exploration or production well
The EPA must revise the list within 365 days after issuing the rule to include categories and subcategories of major sources.

topic.name: Family-Based Immigration Reform
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative efforts to amend immigration laws in the United States, particularly regarding family-sponsored immigration and the allocation of visas.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) to establish the fiscal year worldwide level of family-sponsored immigrants at 480,000
Revises the definition of 'immediate relative' to include a child, spouse, or parent of a U.S. citizen.
Document 2: Establishes the fiscal year worldwide level of family-sponsored immigrants at 480,000 plus: (1) the previous year's unused visas
Increases immigration visas for unmarried sons and daughters of U.S. citizens.
Document 3: The bill establishes the fiscal year worldwide level of family-sponsored immigrants at 480,000 plus: (1) the previous year's unused visas
The bill redefines 'immediate relative' to mean a child, spouse, permanent partner, or parent of a U.S. citizen.
Document 4: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) to establish the fiscal year worldwide level of family-sponsored immigrants at 480,000
Increases immigration visas for brothers and sisters of U.S. citizens.

topic.name: Emergency Unemployment Compensation
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments and extensions to the Emergency Unemployment Compensation program, detailing eligibility criteria and benefit calculations for individuals affected by unemployment.

Document 1: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 with respect to the state-established individual emergency unemployment compensation account (EUCA).
Requires a further additional Tier-5 period for deposits to an individual's EUCA.
Document 2: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 to revise the formula for Tier-1 amounts a state credits to an applicant's emergency unemployment compensation account (EUCA).
Extends the period of emergency unemployment compensation through August 27, 2009.
Document 3: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 with respect to the state-established individual emergency unemployment compensation account (EUCA).
Extends the program until September 22, 2012.
Document 4: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 to revise the formula for Tier-1 amounts a state credits to an applicant's emergency unemployment compensation account (EUCA).
Provides an additional Tier-2 period for deposits to an individual's EUCA.
Document 5: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 with respect to the state-established individual emergency unemployment compensation account (EUCA).
Requires a further additional Tier-5 period for deposits to an individual's EUCA.

topic.name: U.S. Policy on Syria and Iran
topic.description: This cluster discusses U.S. legislative measures and policies aimed at addressing the conflicts in Syria and Iran, emphasizing humanitarian assistance, sanctions, and support for democratic transitions.

Document 1: condemns the violence and human rights violations perpetrated against the Syrian people
support efforts by democratically oriented opposition groups to agree upon a political transition plan
Document 3: U.S. policy that all diplomatic and coercive economic means should be used to compel the government of Bashar al Assad
impose specified entry and U.S.-based property sanctions against a foreign person that knowingly provided significant financial, material, or technological support to Syria
Document 6: impose U.S.-based property blocking and entry sanctions against any foreign person that is responsible for or complicit in actions that threaten the peace, security, or stability of Iraq or Syria
the government of Syria is in violation of numerous U.N. Security Council Resolutions regarding the provision of humanitarian assistance to the people of Syria
Document 7: maintains certain U.S. restrictions against the government of Syria and related persons until the President certifies that Syria has ended support for terrorism

imposes specified export, financial, procurement, banking, and property sanctions on persons that transfer goods or technology contributing to Syria's biological, chemical, nuclear, or advanced conventional weapons programs

topic.name: Veteran and Military Family Support
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing benefits and support for veterans and their families, particularly concerning health care, disability compensation, and family leave.

Document 1: Outlines Department of Defense (DOD) and Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) roles with respect to the retirement for disability of members of the Armed Forces.
Presumes post-traumatic stress disorder to be service-connected, and therefore compensable under veterans' disability compensation provisions.
Document 2: Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993 to revise its requirements for exigency leave with respect to employees belonging to the family of members of the Armed Forces.
Defines a veteran's serious injury or illness of a veteran to be one incurred by the veteran as an Armed Forces member in the line of duty.
Document 4: Directs the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to establish a program to provide former members of the Armed Forces with an initial mental health assessment.
The VA may provide such mental health care services pursuant to a contract with a qualified mental health professional.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Defense and the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to develop a single medical examination for members of the Armed Forces.
Provides extended benefits under TRICARE for the primary caregivers of members who incur a serious injury or illness while on active duty.
Document 6: Requires a member of the Armed Forces who is medically retired because of physical disability incurred in or aggravated by military service to be treated as an active-duty member.

Directs the Secretaries of Defense and Veterans Affairs to establish a joint office for the Department of Defense (DOD) and VA for the coordination of assistance to members in their transition from military service to civilian life.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of each military department to provide each reserve member of that department who is being evaluated for separation or retirement for disability the option to remain on active duty.
Encourages the use of local residences by reserve members during such transition periods.

topic.name: Health Care Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at improving health care access, delivery, and payment systems in the United States.

Document 1: Medicare Access and CHIP Reauthorization Act of 2015 TITLE I--SGR REPEAL AND MEDICARE PROVIDER PAYMENT MODERNIZATION
removes sustainable growth rate (SGR) methodology from the determination of annual conversion factors
Document 2: Medical Efficiency and Delivery Improvement of Care Act (MEDIC) of 2009
to establish a program to provide loans to eligible hospitals to establish residency training programs
Document 3: Medicare Improvements for Patients and Providers Act of 2008
to cover additional preventive services
Document 4: Indian Health Care Improvement Act Amendments of 2008
to raise the health status of Indians and Urban Indians by 2010
Document 5: Indian Health Care Improvement Act Amendments of 2007
to declare a new national Indian health policy
Document 6: Indian Health Care Improvement Reauthorization and Extension Act of 2009
to authorize appropriations to carry out the Act
Document 7: Children's Health and Medicare Protection (CHAMP) Act of 2007
to provide dependable and stable funding for children's health insurance

topic.name: U.S. Government Funding Initiatives
topic.description: Documents related to various U.S. government funding initiatives and appropriations for specific programs and projects.

Document 1: Prohibits funds from being used to carry out the Ambassador's Fund for Cultural Preservation
any other program that provides grants for cultural preservation outside the United States.
Document 2: Urges the President to make every effort, in conjunction with the government of Israel, the Palestinian Authority, and the international community to establish an International Fund for Israeli-Palestinian Peace.
Authorizes appropriations for U.S. contributions to the Fund.
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to authorize appropriations for the state criminal alien assistance program (SCAAP) through FY2015.
Document 4: Authorizes appropriations for FY2008-FY2012 to fund 1,000 additional Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) field agents.
Document 5: Authorizes the Administrator of the Maritime Administration to accept a gift of money from the U.S. Merchant Marine Academy Alumni Association and Foundation, Inc.
to renovate Melville Hall on the campus of the U.S. Merchant Marine Academy.
Document 6: Designates the composition consisting of the words and music known as 'America the Beautiful' as the national anthem.
Document 7: Authorizes the Secretary of the Air Force to alter a hangar at Holloman Air Force Base, New Mexico, using amounts authorized to be appropriated for FY2009 under this Act.

topic.name: Data Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at enhancing data privacy, security, and the regulation of background checks in relation to sensitive personally identifiable information.

Document 1: Amends the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act to repeal the contingent conditions under which the National Association of Registered Agents and Brokers (NARAB) shall not be established.
Requires an individual insurance producer to undergo a criminal history record check by the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI).
Document 2: Establishes policies and procedures to streamline the process of obtaining nationwide background checks.
Defines a 'covered individual' as someone who may have unsupervised access to a child served by a covered entity.
Document 3: Defines 'sensitive personally identifiable information' to include specified combinations of data elements in electronic or digital form.
Imposes a prison term of up to five years for fraud in connection with unauthorized access of personally identifiable information.
Document 4: Defines 'sensitive personally identifiable information' to include specified combinations of data elements in electronic or digital form.
Increases penalties for fraud and related activity, and imposes criminal penalties for attempts and conspiracies to commit fraud.
Document 5: Defines 'sensitive personally identifiable information' for purposes of this Act to include specified combinations of data elements in electronic or digital form.
Grants the U.S. Secret Service and the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) authority to investigate criminal concealments of security breaches.
Document 6: Imposes a fine and/or prison term of up to five years for intentionally or willfully concealing a security breach involving sensitive personally identifiable information.
Makes it unlawful for a service provider to knowingly or intentionally redirect web searches without the consent of the Internet user.
Document 7: Imposes a fine and/or prison term of up to five years for intentionally or willfully concealing a security breach.
Makes any interstate business entity that collects sensitive personally identifiable information subject to data privacy and security program requirements.

topic.name: Advanced Vehicle Technologies and Deployment
topic.description: This cluster focuses on the development, research, and deployment of advanced vehicle technologies, particularly electric and alternative fuel vehicles, and associated infrastructure to reduce petroleum use and emissions.

Document 1: national plug-in electric drive vehicle deployment program
clean energy standard that promotes the use of renewable and other low-carbon sources of electricity
Document 2: national plug-in electric drive vehicle deployment program
provide technical assistance upon request to state, local, and tribal governments
Document 3: research, development, demonstration, and commercial application of vehicles
substantially reduce or eliminate petroleum use and related emissions
Document 4: developing plug-in electric drive and advanced technology vehicles
supporting smart grid technology and peak demand reduction goals
Document 5: national plug-in electric drive vehicle deployment program
develop a national oil independence plan
Document 6: research, development, demonstration, and commercial application activities on materials, technologies, and processes
achieve deep reductions in petroleum use and emissions
Document 7: conduct a program of basic and applied research
substantially reduce or eliminate petroleum use by, and emissions from, the nation's passenger and commercial vehicles

topic.name: FDA User Fee Programs
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments and acts related to the establishment and reauthorization of user fee programs by the FDA for prescription drugs, generic drugs, biosimilars, and animal drugs.

Document 1: reauthorize and establish new Food and Drug Administration (FDA) prescription drug user-fee programs
requires the Secretary to prepare and submit to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce and the Senate Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions
Document 2: direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS), beginning FY2013, to assess and collect the following fees related to generic drugs
requires the Secretary to report to Congress on the progress of the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) in achieving specified safety, access, and transparency goals
Document 3: extend for FY2014-FY2018 the authority of the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to collect generic animal drug user fees
requires the FDA to report to Congress on achieving goals related to the generic animal drug development
Document 4: reauthorizes prescription drug user fees beginning in FY2008
requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to provide a partial refund of an applicant's user fees if the application is withdrawn
Document 5: reauthorize and establish new Food and Drug Administration (FDA) prescription drug user-fee programs
directs the Secretary to assess and collect human generic drug user fees through FY2017
Document 6: reauthorizes prescription drug user fees beginning in FY2008
sets forth provisions regarding adjustments to such fees
Document 7: authorized prescription drug fees be dedicated toward expediting the drug development process
requires the Secretary to present to Congress recommendations developed for achieving certain goals

topic.name: Health Insurance Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts related to health insurance coverage, patient rights, and restrictions on federal funding for specific health services.

Document 1: Repeals provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (PPACA) and the Health Care and Education Reconciliation Act of 2010 relating to health insurance.
Qualified health plan is expanded to include any health plan.
Document 2: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 to require a group or individual health plan that provides medical and surgical benefits to ensure coverage for breast cancer treatment.
Prohibits such a plan from restricting benefits for any hospital length of stay to less than 48 hours in connection with a mastectomy.
Document 3: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 to ensure that inpatient coverage and radiation therapy are provided for breast cancer treatment.
Prohibits a health plan from taking specified actions to avoid the requirements of this Act.
Document 4: Permits individuals to continue their individual health insurance coverage in which they are enrolled on December 31, 2013.
Amends PPACA to require health insurance issuers to provide notice regarding coverage.
Document 5: Prohibits federal funds from being expended for abortion or health coverage that includes coverage of abortion.
Requires disclosure of a plan's coverage of abortion at the time of enrollment.
Document 6: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 to ensure coverage for breast cancer treatment.
Requires a group health plan to provide notice regarding the coverage required under this Act.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to disqualify amounts paid for an abortion from the tax deduction for medical expenses.
Excludes from the definition of 'qualified health plan' any plan that includes coverage for abortion.

topic.name: Small Business Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving access to capital and support for small businesses in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act to increase temporarily the maximum Small Business Administration (SBA) participation in a loan
Raises from $1.5 million to $4.5 million the ceiling on the total amount outstanding and committed to the borrower
Document 2: Amends the Small Business Act to direct the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish and carry out a program to provide support to SBA regional, district, and branch offices
Directs the Administrator to carry out a rural lending outreach program to provide loans of up to $250,000 to rural lenders
Document 3: This bill amends the Small Business Act to revise requirements for increasing prime federal contracting opportunities for small business concerns
The SBA shall include in such annual reports awards to these small business concerns that were purchased by another entity after the initial contract was awarded

topic.name: Harmonized Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: Documents discussing modifications and temporary suspensions of duties on various goods in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: modify the article description of certain men's footwear
extend the temporary suspension of duty on such footwear
Document 2: extend the temporary suspension of duty on phenyl (4,6-dimethoxy-pyrimidin-2-yl) carbamate
Document 3: temporarily suspend the duty on certain leathered footwear for men
Document 4: suspend temporarily the duty on mixtures containing methyl 4-({[(3-methoxy-4-methyl-5-oxo-4,5-dihydro-1H-1,2,4-triazol-1-yl)carbonyl]-amino}sulfonyl)-5-methylthiophene-3-carboxylate
Document 5: suspend temporarily the duty on mixtures of phosphonium, tetrakis(hydroxymethyl)-, chloride, polymer with urea
Document 6: suspend temporarily the duty on certain leather upper sports footwear
Document 7: extend the temporary suspension of duty on ethofumesate in bulk or mixtures

topic.name: Health Research and Quality Care
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts aimed at improving health research, education, and quality care for chronic illnesses and diseases, focusing on patient-centered approaches and addressing health disparities.

Document 1: Authorizes appropriations for conducting and supporting research on Alzheimer's disease
Requires the Director to conduct research related to early detection, diagnosis, and prevention of Alzheimer's disease.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to conduct a national evidence-based education campaign
Encourage young women to be aware of their personal risk factors, strategies and methods for increasing early detection.
Document 3: Requires the Director of NIH to provide for ongoing research and other activities with respect to pre-diabetes and diabetes in minority populations
Conduct research on diabetes in minority populations.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to convene a Patient-Centered Health Care and Quality of Life Stakeholder Strategic Summit
Award grants to increase demand for and delivery of integrated, patient-centered care.
Document 5: Amends the Public Health Service Act to improve the quality of cancer care and the quality of life for cancer patients
Establish a Quality of Life Advisory Committee to advise, coordinate, and assist the CDC.
Document 6: Establish a national quality of life education and awareness grants program initiative for seriously ill patients
Encouraging an increased demand for and delivery of integrated and patient-centered care.
Document 7: Analyze key health system barriers to providing patient-centered health care
Identify strategic solutions for addressing quality of life concerns for patients facing serious illnesses.

topic.name: Federal Recognition of Virginia Tribes
topic.description: This topic encompasses the legislative acts aimed at extending federal recognition to various Indian tribes in Virginia, detailing their rights, benefits, and governance.

Document 1: Extends federal recognition to the following Indian tribes of Virginia
Makes the tribes and their members eligible for all services and benefits provided by the federal government to federally recognized Indian tribes.
Document 2: Extends federal recognition to the following Indian tribes of Virginia
Makes each Tribe and tribal members eligible for all services and benefits provided by the federal government to federally recognized Indian tribes.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to accept title to any additional acres of real property
the real property taken into trust is not to be eligible, or used, for Class II or III gaming.
Document 4: Extends federal recognition to the Chickahominy Tribe
Makes the tribe and its members eligible for all services and benefits provided by the federal government.
Document 5: Extends federal recognition to the Chickahominy Tribe
Makes the Tribe and tribal members eligible for all federal services and benefits provided to federally recognized Indian tribes.
Document 6: Extends federal recognition to the Chickahominy Tribe
Makes the Tribe and tribal members eligible for all federal services and benefits provided to federally recognized Indian tribes.
Document 7: Extends federal recognition to the Chickahominy Tribe
Makes the Tribe and tribal members eligible for all federal services and benefits provided to federally recognized Indian tribes.

topic.name: Federal Election Campaign Reforms
topic.description: Legislation aimed at amending the Federal Election Campaign Act to enhance transparency, accountability, and fairness in federal election financing.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971
require every person who is an independent political actor... to file a registration statement with the Federal Election Commission (FEC)
Document 2: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 (FECA) with respect to candidate benefits of fair elections financing
establishment of the Fair Elections Fund and of a Fair Elections Oversight Board
Document 3: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more
Requires Senate candidates to file designations, statements, and reports directly with the FEC
Document 4: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 (FECA) with respect to eligibility and qualifying contribution requirements
establishment of a Fair Elections Fund
Document 5: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more
Requires Senate candidates to file designations, statements, and reports directly with the FEC
Document 6: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 (FECA) with respect to candidate benefits of fair elections financing
civil penalties for violation of contribution and expenditure requirements
Document 7: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to limit to $50,000 in a federal election the aggregate contributions any person may make to any candidate
Requires each political committee to post mandatory contribution information on its public website

topic.name: U.S. Defense and Education Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation related to the authorization of appropriations for defense and education programs in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 (HEA) to revise HEA programs and to extend the authorization of appropriations for them through FY2013.
Revises the definition of institution of higher education (IHE) to include schools that award degrees.
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations for FY2011 for the Army, Navy and Marine Corps, and Air Force for aircraft, missiles, weapons and tracked combat vehicles, ammunition, shipbuilding and conversion, and other procurement.
Prohibits the Secretary of Defense from procuring more than two brigade sets of early-infantry brigade combat team increment one equipment.
Document 3: Authorizes appropriations for FY2010 for the Army, Navy and Marine Corps, and Air Force for aircraft, missiles, weapons and tracked combat vehicles, ammunition, shipbuilding and conversion, and other procurement.
Decreases by $1.75 billion the amount authorized for Air Force procurement.
Document 4: Authorizes appropriations for FY2010 for defense-wide procurement; National Guard and reserve equipment.
Prohibits the Secretary of the Navy from obligating or expending funds for construction of, or advance procurement of materials for, a surface combatant vessel.
Document 5: Authorizes appropriations for FY2008 for the Army, Navy and Marine Corps, and Air Force for aircraft, missiles, weapons and tracked combat vehicles, ammunition, shipbuilding and conversion, the Joint Improvised Explosive Device Defeat Fund, and other procurement.
Prohibits the use of specified Army procurement funds to close the production line for the Army Tactical Missile System program.
Document 6: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 (HEA) to revise and reauthorize HEA programs.
Revises the definition of an institution of higher education (IHE) to include schools that admit students whose secondary education was conducted in a home school setting.

topic.name: Medicare Home Infusion Therapy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the coverage and implementation of home infusion therapy and associated services under Medicare.

Document 1: prohibit payment at the applicable site neutral payment rate for discharges
study the treatment needs of individuals entitled to benefits under Medicare part A
Document 2: provide for Medicare coverage of home infusion therapy and home infusion drugs
ensure that Medicare beneficiaries have timely and appropriate access to infusion therapy in their homes
Document 3: require a prescription drug plan (PDP) sponsor to have in place a payment structure for covered part D drugs
encourages the use of dispensing techniques that foster efficiency
Document 4: authorize Medicare coverage of home infusion therapy and home infusion drugs
rapid and seamless coordination between drug coverage under Medicare part D
Document 5: provide for Medicare coverage of home infusion therapy and home infusion drugs
ensure that Medicare beneficiaries have timely and appropriate access to infusion therapy in their homes
Document 6: authorize Medicare coverage of home infusion therapy and home infusion drugs
ensure that Medicare beneficiaries have timely and appropriate access to infusion therapy in their homes
Document 7: authorize Medicare coverage of home infusion therapy and home infusion drugs
ensure that Medicare beneficiaries have timely and appropriate access to infusion therapy in their homes

topic.name: Banking and Insurance Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses the legislative measures related to capital requirements, regulatory standards, and accountability in the banking and insurance sectors.

Document 1: Amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act concerning establishment of minimum leverage and minimum risk-based capital requirements

Exempts from any requirement to prepare holding company financial statements in accordance with Generally Accepted Accounting Principles any Board-supervised depository institution holding company or nonbank financial company that is also a person regulated by a state insurance regulator.
Document 2: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 to prohibit a bank holding company from holding more than 10% of the total amount of deposits of insured depository institutions in the United States

Prescribes minimum leverage ratios (6% of average total consolidated assets) and balance sheet leverage ratios (6% of tier 1 capital for all outstanding balance sheet liabilities) for tier 1 capital maintained by a bank holding company or financial company.
Document 3: Amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act concerning establishment of minimum leverage and minimum risk-based capital requirements

States that a Board-supervised depository institution holding company or nonbank financial company engaged in the insurance business and regulated by either a state insurance regulator or the National Association of Insurance Commissioners...
Document 4: Amends the Truth in Lending Act, as amended by the Credit CARD Act of 2009, to prohibit a creditor from imposing upon an open-end consumer credit account any fee related to failure of the consumer to...
Requires any annual fee that a card issuer may impose with respect to a credit card account under an open-end consumer credit plan to be reasonable.
Document 5: Amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act and the Home Owners' Loan Act to make minimum leverage and risk-based capital requirements governing insurance companies under the state law also apply to insurance companies that are either depository holding companies or subsidiaries of depository holding companies.

Prohibits the Board from requiring a nonbank financial company that is an insurance company under its supervision to comply with accounting standards that differ from regulatory accounting standards under state law.
Document 6: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 to prohibit a bank holding company from holding more than 10% of the total amount of deposits of insured depository institutions in the United States

Prescribes leverage ratio requirements for operating subsidiaries of bank holding companies and financial companies to prohibit either a bank holding company with total consolidated assets of $50 billion or more...
Document 7: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 to prohibit a bank holding company from holding more than 10% of the total amount of deposits of insured depository institutions in the United States
Prohibits a financial company from holding more than 10% of the total consolidated liabilities of all financial companies.

topic.name: FAA and Transportation Funding
topic.description: This cluster discusses various acts related to the authorization and funding of transportation programs, particularly those involving the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) and other transportation security and safety measures.

Document 1: reauthorizes through FY2017 the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA)
airport improvement program; air navigation facilities and equipment
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations to the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) for FY2010 and FY2011 for operations
grants for airport planning and development
Document 3: Authorizes appropriations to the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) out of the Airport and Airway Trust Fund for FY2012-FY2015
Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) air navigation facilities and equipment
Document 4: Authorizes appropriations to the Secretary of Homeland Security (Secretary) for the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) for FY2010- FY2011
directs the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security (TSA) to report on TSA use of a risk-based system
Document 5: Authorizes appropriations for FY2009-FY2013 for railroad safety
directs the Secretary of Transportation (Secretary) to develop a long-term railroad strategy for improving railroad safety
Document 6: Authorizes FY2011 appropriations for the Coast Guard
research, development, test, and evaluation of technologies, materials, and human factors relating to the Coast Guard's mission
Document 7: Authorizes appropriations for FY2008 for the Coast Guard
acquisition, construction, rebuilding, and improvement of aids to navigation

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Programs
topic.description: The documents discuss various agricultural policies and programs aimed at supporting farmers through financial assistance, risk management, and food security initiatives.

Document 1: Establishes the agriculture risk coverage program through crop year 2018 for covered crops to make payments to producers
Authorizes nonrecourse marketing assistance loans, loan deficiency payments, and programs for upland cotton and extra long staple cotton.
Document 2: Amends the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act to require the Secretary of Agriculture to provide performance awards to states
Establishes a demonstration project to determine the effectiveness of directly certifying children for free school meals using household income data from Medicaid.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to provide for a farm's base acreage adjustment for covered commodities
Provides for crop years 2014-2018 either price loss coverage if a covered commodity's effective price is less than its reference price.
Document 4: Establishes the agriculture risk coverage program for crop years 2014-2018
Makes adverse market payments available for the 2014-2018 crop years to producers on farms where the actual price for a covered commodity is less than the reference price.
Document 5: Defines specified terms related to commodity programs
Requires election of either direct payments and counter-cyclical payments or conservation payments.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (Secretary) to adjust base acres for covered commodities for a farm
Prohibits a producer from receiving direct payments, counter-cyclical payments, or average crop revenue election payments if a farm's base acreage is 10 acres or less.
Document 7: Repeals direct payments under the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to notify farm owners regarding their opportunity to elect to retain base acres.

topic.name: Tax Reform Legislation
topic.description: A collection of legislative acts aimed at modifying the Internal Revenue Code to adjust tax rates, deductions, and credits for individual and corporate taxpayers.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code with respect to individual taxpayers to reduce to three income tax brackets
Imposes a flat tax of 35 percent on corporate taxable income.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to estate, gift, and generation-skipping transfer taxes
Impose an increased tax for estates over $3.5 million.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend expiring provisions relating to the alternative minimum tax
Extends through 2008 for individual taxpayers the offset of nonrefundable personal tax credits against regular and alternative minimum tax.
Document 4: Extends through 2013 for an individual taxpayer the tax rate reductions and other tax benefits
Modifies individual income tax brackets for 2013 to reduce income tax for taxpayers whose adjusted gross income is less than the applicable threshold amount.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2014 the tax credit for purchasing health care insurance
Extends through 2014 provisions allowing continuation of health care benefits for eligible trade adjustment assistance beneficiaries.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend expiring provisions relating to the alternative minimum tax
Extends through 2009 various business-related tax provisions.
Document 7: Amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to individual income tax rates
Extends through 2008 various expiring tax provisions primarily affecting individual taxpayers.

topic.name: Tax Deductions and Amendments
topic.description: This cluster discusses various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code related to tax deductions for individuals and businesses.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the tax deduction for income attributable to domestic production activities in Puerto Rico.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the special rule allowing a tax deduction for charitable contributions of food inventories by business taxpayers.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase from 50 to 80% the percentage of meals and entertainment expenses that may be claimed as a business tax deduction.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow all individual taxpayers to deduct from gross income state, local, and foreign real property taxes.

topic.name: U.S. Trade and Economic Policy
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts related to trade, economic sanctions, and financial regulations in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Trade Act of 1974 to extend duty-free treatment under the Generalized System of Preferences (GSP) through July 31, 2013.
Amends the Trade and Globalization Adjustment Assistance Act of 2009 to repeal the December 31, 2011, termination date for trade adjustment assistance (TAA) programs.
Document 2: Expresses the sense of Congress that the goal of compelling Iran to abandon efforts to acquire a nuclear weapons capability can be achieved through a policy that includes economic sanctions.

Amends the Iran Sanctions Act of 1996 to increase from three to five the minimum number of sanctions that the President is required to impose for violations relating to the development of Iran's petroleum resources.
Document 3: Amends the Securities Act of 1933 to define 'emerging growth company' as an issuer that had total annual gross revenues of less than $1 billion.
Exempts emerging growth companies from the requirement for separate shareholder approval of executive compensation.
Document 4: Prohibits oil and gas preleasing or leasing of any area of the Outer Continental Shelf that was not available for oil and gas leasing as of July 1, 2008.
Requires approval by law of an adjacent state for oil or gas leasing in any area located within 100 miles from the adjacent state's coastline.
Document 5: Directs the Federal Mortgage Insurance Corporation to take all steps necessary to dissolve and eliminate the Federal National Mortgage Association and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation.
Establishes the FMIC as an independent agency to facilitate a liquid, transparent, and resilient single-family and multifamily mortgage credit market.
Document 6: Amends the Trade Act of 1974 and the African Growth and Opportunity Act to extend through FY2025 the duty-free treatment of the products of beneficiary sub-Saharan African countries.

The President must not terminate the designation of a country as a beneficiary sub-Saharan African country unless, at least 60 days before termination, the President notifies Congress and the country of that intention.
Document 7: Establishes the Financial Stability Oversight Council to identify risks to U.S. financial stability.
Requires the Council to promote market discipline by eliminating expectations that the Government will shield shareholders from losses in the event of failure.

topic.name: Designation of Federal Buildings
topic.description: This cluster discusses the legislative measures that designate various federal buildings and post offices in the United States in honor of individuals.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 11662 Gravois Road in St. Louis, Missouri, as the 'Lt. Daniel P. Riordan Post Office.'
Document 2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 229 West Main Cross Street, in Findlay, Ohio, as the 'Michael Garver Oxley Memorial Post Office Building.'
Document 3: Designates the U.S. courthouse located at 200 NW 4th Street in Oklahoma City, Oklahoma, as the 'William J. Holloway, Jr. United States Courthouse.'
Document 4: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 450 Lexington Avenue in New York, New York, as the 'Vincent R. Sombrotto Post Office.'
Document 5: Designates the federal building located at 617 Walnut Street in Helena, Arkansas, as the 'Jacob Trieber Federal Building, United States Post Office, and United States Court House.'
Document 6: Designates the United States courthouse under construction, as of the enactment of this Act, at 327 South Church Street, Rockford, Illinois, as the 'Stanley J. Roszkowski United States Courthouse.'
Document 7: Designates the U.S. courthouse located at 501 East Court Street in Jackson, Mississippi, as the 'R. Jess Brown United States Courthouse.'

topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Acts
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative amendments and acts addressing diverse issues such as public health, environmental protection, and national memorials.

Document 1: provides assistance to combat HIV/AIDS in India
Amends the State Department Basic Authorities Act of 1956
Document 2: Amends the Neotropical Migratory Bird Conservation Act
to increase the amount authorized to be appropriated for FY2010
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to refund a portion of the excise tax
paid by certain coal producers on coal exported
Document 4: Amends the Marine Mammal Protection Act of 1972
to prohibit issuing permits for the importation of polar bear parts
Document 5: requires that the naturalization oath of allegiance be the same formulation
as the formulation of the oath administered on July 20, 2015
Document 6: Amends the Federal Crop Insurance Act
to reduce crop insurance assistance for crops grown on native sod acreage
Document 7: Authorizes the World War I Memorial Foundation to establish a commemorative work
rededicating the District of Columbia War Memorial
